[14] never veering right or left from any of these words I'm commanding you today, never running off after other gods to serve them.

[15] But if you will not listen to the voice of the LORD your God — if you do not guard and live out every command and statute I'm giving you today — then all these curses will hunt you down and overtake you:

[16] Cursed in the city. Cursed in the field.

[17] Cursed: your basket and your kneading bowl.

[18] Cursed: the children of your body, the harvest of your soil, the young of your herds, the lambs of your flocks.

[19] Cursed when you walk in. Cursed when you walk out.

[20] The LORD will turn loose on you a curse, a panic, a withering — on every venture your hand takes up — until you are destroyed, until you vanish in a hurry, because of the evil you've done in abandoning him.

[21] The LORD will fasten plague to you until it has wiped you off the land you are entering to possess.

[22] The LORD will strike you with consumption, with fever, with inflammation, with scorching heat and drought, with blight and mildew. They will chase you until you perish.

[23] The sky above your head will turn to bronze. The ground beneath your feet, to iron.

[24] The LORD will turn the rain of your land into dust and ash — falling from the sky on you until you are destroyed.

[25] The LORD will hand you over, broken, to your enemies. You'll march at them down one road and scatter from them down seven. You will become a horror to every kingdom on earth.

[26] Your corpses will be food for every bird in the sky and every beast on the ground — and no one will frighten them away.

[27] The LORD will strike you with the boils of Egypt, with tumors, with running sores, with the itch — none of which can be cured.

[28] The LORD will strike you with madness, with blindness, with the panic of a soul coming apart.

[29] At noon you will grope like a blind man fumbling in the dark. Nothing you do will succeed. You will be robbed and beaten down every single day, with no one to rescue you.

[30] You will get engaged to a woman — another man will rape her. You will build a house — you will never live in it. You will plant a vineyard — you will never taste its fruit.

[31] Your ox slaughtered before your eyes — and you'll get nothing of it. Your donkey ripped away in front of you — never returned. Your flock handed over to your enemies — and no one will rescue you.

[32] Your sons and daughters handed over to a foreign people while you watch — eyes aching for them all day long — and your hand powerless.

[33] A people you've never heard of will eat the harvest your soil produced, everything you worked for. You will be nothing but oppressed and crushed, every day of your life.

[34] You will go insane from what your own eyes are forced to see.

[35] The LORD will strike you on the knees and the legs with boils that cannot be cured — from the soles of your feet to the top of your head.

[36] The LORD will haul you off — you and the king you've set over yourselves — to a nation neither you nor your ancestors have ever known. There you will serve other gods, gods of wood and stone.

[37] You will become a horror story, a punchline, a curse-word among every people the LORD drives you to.

[38] You'll carry mountains of seed out to your field — and gather almost nothing back. The locusts will devour it.

[39] You'll plant vineyards and tend them — but you will not drink the wine, you will not gather the grapes. The worm will eat them.

[40] You'll have olive trees across all your territory — but no oil to anoint yourself. Your olives will drop off the branch.

[41] You'll father sons and daughters — but they will not be yours. They'll be marched off into captivity.

[42] All your trees, all the produce of your soil — the insects will claim it.

[43] The foreigner living among you will climb higher and higher above you, and you will sink lower and lower.

[44] He will lend to you — you'll have nothing to lend him. He will be the head; you will be the tail.

[45] All these curses will come down on you. They will chase you and overtake you and destroy you — because you would not listen to the voice of the LORD your God, would not keep the commands and statutes he gave you.

[46] They will brand you and your descendants as a sign and a wonder forever.

[47] Because you did not serve the LORD your God with joy, with gladness of heart, when everything was overflowing —

[48] you will serve enemies the LORD sends against you, in hunger and thirst and nakedness and total destitution. He will fasten an iron yoke on your neck until he has destroyed you.

[49] The LORD will bring a nation against you from far away, from the ends of the earth — swooping in like an eagle — a nation whose language you will not understand,

[50] hard-faced, with no respect for the old and no mercy for the young.

[51] They will eat the young of your livestock and the harvest of your soil — until you are destroyed. They will leave you no grain, no wine, no oil, no calves, no lambs — until you are gone.

[52] They will lay siege to you in every city across your land, until the high fortified walls you trusted come crashing down. They will trap you in every city, in every corner of the land the LORD your God gave you.

[53] In the siege — in the desperate squeeze your enemies will put you in — you will eat the fruit of your own body, the flesh of the sons and daughters the LORD your God gave you.

[54] Even the most refined, the most tender man among you will turn pitiless toward his own brother, toward the wife in his arms, toward the children he has left —

[55] refusing to share with any of them the flesh of his own children he is eating, because nothing else is left to him in the siege, in the squeeze your enemies will put on you in every city.

[56] The most refined, most delicate woman among you — the kind who would not put the sole of her foot on the ground because she was so delicate — will turn pitiless toward the husband in her arms, toward her own son, her own daughter,

[57] hiding the afterbirth that came from between her legs, hiding the children she just bore — to eat them in secret — because nothing else is left in the siege, in the squeeze your enemies will put on you in your cities.

[58] If you will not guard and live out every word of this teaching written in this scroll — if you will not fear this glorious and terrifying name, the LORD your God —

[59] then the LORD will strike you and your descendants with plagues out of nightmare: relentless, savage, and lingering — diseases that drag on and refuse to die.

[60] He will bring back on you every sickness of Egypt that terrified you, and they will cling to you.

[61] Every disease and every plague not even written in this scroll — the LORD will pile them on you until you are destroyed.

[62] You who were once as many as the stars of the sky will be left a handful — because you would not listen to the voice of the LORD your God.

[63] Just as the LORD once delighted to make you flourish and multiply, he will now delight to ruin you and wipe you out. You will be torn off the land you are entering to possess.

[64] The LORD will scatter you among every people, from one end of the earth to the other. There you will serve other gods — gods of wood and stone — gods neither you nor your ancestors ever knew.

[65] Among those nations you will find no rest, no place for the sole of your foot to come down. The LORD will give you there a trembling heart, eyes wasting away, a soul running on empty.

[66] Your life will dangle by a thread in front of you. You will be terrified, night and day, and never sure you'll live to see morning.

[67] In the morning you'll say, "If only it were night." In the night you'll say, "If only it were morning." — because of the dread shaking your heart and the things your eyes are forced to see.

[68] The LORD will haul you back to Egypt in ships — by the very road I told you you would never see again. There you will offer yourselves up as slaves to your enemies — and no one will even buy you.

[1] These are the terms of the covenant the LORD ordered Moses to cut with Israel in the land of Moab—on top of the covenant he had already cut with them at Horeb.

[2] Moses summoned all Israel and said to them: You watched it with your own eyes—everything the LORD did in Egypt to Pharaoh, to his officials, to his whole land.

[3] The brutal tests your eyes witnessed, those staggering signs, those impossible wonders.

[4] But to this day the LORD has not given you a mind that grasps it, eyes that truly see, ears that actually hear.

[5] Forty years I marched you through the wilderness. Your clothes never wore out. The sandals on your feet never gave way.

[6] You ate no bread. You drank no wine, no beer. All so you would know: I am the LORD your God.

[7] When you arrived at this place, Sihon king of Heshbon and Og king of Bashan came out to fight us—and we crushed them.

[8] We seized their land and handed it over to Reuben, Gad, and the half-tribe of Manasseh as their inheritance.

[9] So keep the words of this covenant. Do them. Then you will succeed in everything you set your hand to.

[10] Today every one of you stands here before the LORD your God—your tribal chiefs, your elders, your officials, every man of Israel,

[11] your children, your wives, and the foreigners camped among you, from the woodcutter to the water-carrier—

[12] standing here to enter into covenant with the LORD your God, sworn under oath, the covenant the LORD your God is cutting with you today,

[13] so he can establish you today as his people and be your God—just as he promised you, just as he swore to your ancestors, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.

[14] And I am not making this covenant, this sworn agreement, with you alone.

[15] I am making it with everyone standing here today before the LORD our God—and with everyone who is not here with us today.

[16] You know how we lived in Egypt. You know how we passed through the nations on the way here.

[17] You saw their vile things—their gods of wood and stone, silver and gold, propped up among them.

[18] Make sure there is no one here—no man, no woman, no clan, no tribe—whose heart is already turning away from the LORD our God to go worship the gods of those nations. Make sure no root grows among you that bears poison and bitterness as its fruit.

[19] When such a person hears the words of this oath, he congratulates himself in his heart: I'll be fine—I can walk in the stubbornness of my own heart. As if drenching the dry ground along with the wet would somehow save him.

[20] The LORD will not forgive that man. The LORD's anger and jealousy will burn against him like fire. Every curse written in this book will settle on him. The LORD will erase his name from under heaven.

[21] The LORD will single him out of all the tribes of Israel for disaster, in line with every curse of the covenant written in this scroll of instruction.

[22] Then the generations to come—your children who rise up after you, and the foreigner who arrives from a distant land—they will see the catastrophe inflicted on this land, the diseases the LORD has unleashed on it,

[23] and they will say: The whole land—burning sulfur, salt, scorched earth. Nothing planted. Nothing sprouting. Not a blade of green. Like the overthrow of Sodom and Gomorrah, Admah and Zeboiim, the cities the LORD demolished in his fury.

[24] And all the nations will ask: Why did the LORD do this to this land? Why this blazing, consuming rage?

[25] And the answer will come: Because they abandoned the covenant of the LORD, the God of their ancestors—the covenant he cut with them when he brought them out of Egypt.

[26] They went and served other gods. They bowed to gods they had never known, gods he had never assigned to them.

[27] So the LORD's anger ignited against this land. He poured down on it every curse written in this book.

[28] In burning fury, in towering wrath, the LORD ripped them from their soil and hurled them into another land—where they are to this day.

[29] The hidden things belong to the LORD our God. But what he has revealed belongs to us and our children forever—so we can live out every word of this instruction.

[1] When all of this lands on you — the blessing and the curse I've laid before you — and you're scattered among the nations where the LORD your God has driven you,

[2] and you come back to him, you and your children, with everything in you, listening to his voice the way I'm commanding you today —

[3] then the LORD your God will turn your exile around. He'll have compassion on you. He'll gather you back from every nation where he scattered you.

[4] Even if you've been flung to the far edge of the sky, he'll reach there and pull you back.

[5] He'll bring you home — to the land your ancestors held — and you'll possess it again. He'll do you more good than he ever did them. He'll make you more than they ever were.

[6] And the LORD your God will perform surgery on your heart and on your children's hearts — circumcising it so you can love him with everything you are, with every breath in you, and live.

[7] Then he'll turn every one of these curses onto your enemies — onto the ones who hated you, the ones who hunted you.

[8] As for you — you'll come back. You'll listen to the LORD's voice. You'll do everything he commands you today.

[9] And the LORD your God will overflow you with good in everything your hands touch — your children, your livestock, your harvest. He'll take delight in prospering you the way he delighted in your ancestors,

[10] when you listen to the LORD your God's voice, when you keep his commands and statutes written in this book of instruction, when you turn back to him with everything in you.

[11] Look — this command I'm giving you today isn't too hard for you. It isn't out of reach.

[12] It's not up in the sky, so you'd have to say, "Who'll climb up and bring it down so we can hear it and do it?"

[13] It's not across the sea, so you'd have to say, "Who'll cross over and bring it back so we can hear it and do it?"

[14] No — the word is right next to you. It's in your mouth. It's in your heart. You can do this.

[15] Look — today I'm setting before you life and good, death and ruin.

[16] What I'm commanding you today is this: love the LORD your God, walk in his ways, keep his commands and statutes and rulings — and you will live. You will multiply. The LORD your God will bless you in the land you're about to enter and take.

[17] But if your heart turns away — if you stop listening, if you're seduced into bowing down to other gods and serving them —

[18] I'm telling you straight, today: you will perish. You will not last long in the land you're about to cross the Jordan to enter and possess.

[19] I'm calling heaven and earth as witnesses against you today: I have set before you life and death, blessing and curse. Choose life — so you and your children can actually live —

[20] by loving the LORD your God, by listening to his voice, by holding fast to him. Because he is your life. He is the length of your days. He's the reason you'll live in the land the LORD swore to give to your ancestors — to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
